path_cfg_ssa_symbolic.note: Modules exist and are tested, but are not yet invoked in the standard runtime detector pipeline.

findings.title: Minting policy liquidity_mint doesn't validate minted tokens
findings.description: Mint handler never accesses the transaction's mint field. An attacker could mint arbitrary token names under this policy.
findings.suggestion: Check `self.mint` (or `transaction.mint`) to validate token names and quantities.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Unrestricted minting policy liquidity_mint
findings.description: Mint handler has no authorization checks (no signatories, inputs, mint, or reference input validation). Anyone can mint tokens.
findings.suggestion: Add authorization: check `self.extra_signatories` for an admin key, or verify a specific input UTXO is consumed.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).; Exploit scenario generated for runtime simulation; Exploit transaction rejected by validator: UPLC evaluation failed: DeserialisationError("UnBData", Con(Data(Constr(Constr { tag: 121, any_constructor: None, fields: Def([]) })))). This may indicate a false positive for unrestricted-minting.

witness.rejection_error: UPLC evaluation failed: DeserialisationError("UnBData", Con(Data(Constr(Constr { tag: 121, any_constructor: None, fields: Def([]) }))))

findings.title: Lower bound time manipulation risk in orders.spend
findings.description: Handler uses get_lower_bound for time-sensitive logic with field(s) [entered_position_time] but never checks the upper bound. An attacker can set the lower bound arbitrarily far in the past, manipulating time-based calculations (interest accrual, fee computation, deadline checks).
findings.suggestion: Use get_upper_bound instead of get_lower_bound for time-sensitive calculations. The upper bound cannot be set arbitrarily into the past.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).; Exploit scenario generated for runtime simulation; Exploit transaction rejected by validator: UPLC evaluation failed: DeserialisationError("UnBData", Con(Data(Constr(Constr { tag: 121, any_constructor: None, fields: Indef([BigInt(Int(Int(Int { neg: false, val: 1700000000000 })))]) })))). This may indicate a false positive for missing-validity-range.

witness.rejection_error: UPLC evaluation failed: DeserialisationError("UnBData", Con(Data(Constr(Constr { tag: 121, any_constructor: None, fields: Indef([BigInt(Int(Int(Int { neg: false, val: 1700000000000 })))]) }))))

findings.title: Lower bound time manipulation risk in position_mint.mint
findings.description: Handler uses get_lower_bound for time-sensitive logic with field(s) [entered_position_time] but never checks the upper bound. An attacker can set the lower bound arbitrarily far in the past, manipulating time-based calculations (interest accrual, fee computation, deadline checks).
findings.suggestion: Use get_upper_bound instead of get_lower_bound for time-sensitive calculations. The upper bound cannot be set arbitrarily into the past.

findings.title: Unsafe Option datum deconstruction in manage_positions.spend
findings.description: Datum parameter 'datum' is Option<T> but handler never uses `expect Some(..) = datum`. Accessing the datum without safe deconstruction may cause runtime errors.
findings.suggestion: Use `expect Some(datum) = datum_opt` to safely deconstruct the Optional datum.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Handler orders.spend contains division that may fail on zero
findings.description: Division or modulo operation detected. If the denominator is derived from redeemer or datum input, an attacker could cause division by zero, failing the transaction.
findings.suggestion: Guard division with `expect denominator > 0` before the operation.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Handler position_mint.mint contains division that may fail on zero
findings.description: Division or modulo operation detected. If the denominator is derived from redeemer or datum input, an attacker could cause division by zero, failing the transaction.
findings.suggestion: Guard division with `expect denominator > 0` before the operation.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Multiple quantity_of calls in manage_positions.spend may allow double-counting
findings.description: Handler manage_positions.spend calls `quantity_of` 8 times to check asset quantities. Without verifying mutual exclusivity, an attacker may satisfy multiple checks with overlapping assets.
findings.suggestion: Use `value.tokens` or `value.without_lovelace` for exact value matching instead of multiple independent quantity_of checks.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Multiple quantity_of calls in orders.spend may allow double-counting
findings.description: Handler orders.spend calls `quantity_of` 3 times to check asset quantities. Without verifying mutual exclusivity, an attacker may satisfy multiple checks with overlapping assets.
findings.suggestion: Use `value.tokens` or `value.without_lovelace` for exact value matching instead of multiple independent quantity_of checks.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Potential withdraw-zero trick in liquidity_mint.mint
findings.description: Handler liquidity_mint.mint uses withdrawal-based authorization but only checks for withdrawal existence (e.g., dict.has_key), not the amount. An attacker can satisfy this check with a zero withdrawal.
findings.suggestion: Use `dict.get` to retrieve the withdrawal amount and verify it is greater than zero: `expect Some(amount) = dict.get(self.withdrawals, key)` then `amount > 0`.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Potential withdraw-zero trick in pool.spend
findings.description: Handler pool.spend uses withdrawal-based authorization but only checks for withdrawal existence (e.g., dict.has_key), not the amount. An attacker can satisfy this check with a zero withdrawal.
findings.suggestion: Use `dict.get` to retrieve the withdrawal amount and verify it is greater than zero: `expect Some(amount) = dict.get(self.withdrawals, key)` then `amount > 0`.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Redeemer-tainted arithmetic in orders.spend
findings.description: Handler orders.spend uses division and multiplication with redeemer-derived variables (tainted: [redeemer]). The redeemer is attacker-controlled — using it directly in arithmetic risks division-by-zero, overflow, or precision manipulation.
findings.suggestion: Validate redeemer values before arithmetic: `expect redeemer.value > 0` or use datum-sourced values instead.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Redeemer-tainted arithmetic in position_mint.mint
findings.description: Handler position_mint.mint uses division and multiplication with redeemer-derived variables (tainted: [redeemer]). The redeemer is attacker-controlled — using it directly in arithmetic risks division-by-zero, overflow, or precision manipulation.
findings.suggestion: Validate redeemer values before arithmetic: `expect redeemer.value > 0` or use datum-sourced values instead.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Value conservation not verified in manage_positions.spend
findings.description: Handler manage_positions.spend performs value arithmetic and produces outputs but doesn't appear to verify that total value is conserved.
findings.suggestion: Add a check that sum(output_values) == sum(input_values) - fees.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Handler manage_positions.spend doesn't constrain staking credential on outputs
findings.description: Outputs check the payment credential but not the staking credential. An attacker can provide an address with their staking key to steal staking rewards.
findings.suggestion: Also verify `output.address.stake_credential` matches the expected value.

findings.title: Potential cheap spam vulnerability in validator 'manage_positions'
findings.description: Validator 'manage_positions' processes outputs but doesn't enforce minimum value requirements or use an auth token. An attacker could create many small UTXOs at the script address cheaply.
findings.suggestion: Add minimum deposit requirements, require an auth token, or validate output values.

findings.title: Handler manage_positions.spend contains integer subtraction that may underflow
findings.description: Handler manage_positions.spend uses integer subtraction. If the subtrahend is derived from user input (redeemer/datum), the result could go negative, causing unexpected behavior in value calculations.
findings.suggestion: Guard subtraction with `expect b <= a` before computing `a - b`, or use saturating arithmetic patterns.

findings.title: Handler orders.spend contains integer subtraction that may underflow
findings.description: Handler orders.spend uses integer subtraction. If the subtrahend is derived from user input (redeemer/datum), the result could go negative, causing unexpected behavior in value calculations.
findings.suggestion: Guard subtraction with `expect b <= a` before computing `a - b`, or use saturating arithmetic patterns.

findings.title: Datum fields 'liquidity_total_asset_amount', 'total_lended_amount' not validated in pool.spend
findings.description: Handler pool.spend accepts datum type 'PoolDatum' with financial fields 'liquidity_total_asset_amount', 'total_lended_amount' but never accesses them. These fields may be manipulated by an attacker if not validated.
findings.suggestion: Validate datum fields: `expect datum.liquidity_total_asset_amount > 0`, `expect datum.total_lended_amount > 0`

findings.title: Datum fields 'interest_rate', 'max_leverage_factor', 'maintain_margin_amount' not validated in settings.spend
findings.description: Handler settings.spend accepts datum type 'SettingsDatum' with financial fields 'interest_rate', 'max_leverage_factor', 'maintain_margin_amount' but never accesses them. These fields may be manipulated by an attacker if not validated.
findings.suggestion: Validate datum fields: `expect datum.interest_rate > 0`, `expect datum.max_leverage_factor > 0`, `expect datum.maintain_margin_amount > 0`

findings.title: Potential rounding error in orders.spend
findings.description: Handler orders.spend contains both integer division and multiplication. Integer division truncates, so `(a / b) * c` loses precision compared to `(a * c) / b`. Verify the order of operations preserves maximum precision.
findings.suggestion: Reorder operations to multiply before dividing: `a * c / b` instead of `a / b * c`. For complex formulas, consider using a common denominator approach.

findings.title: Potential rounding error in position_mint.mint
findings.description: Handler position_mint.mint contains both integer division and multiplication. Integer division truncates, so `(a / b) * c` loses precision compared to `(a * c) / b`. Verify the order of operations preserves maximum precision.
findings.suggestion: Reorder operations to multiply before dividing: `a * c / b` instead of `a / b * c`. For complex formulas, consider using a common denominator approach.

findings.title: Potential unchecked fee calculation in manage_positions.spend
findings.description: Handler manage_positions.spend performs subtraction with redeemer-derived values and produces outputs. If the subtracted amount (fee, deduction) comes from the redeemer, an attacker can manipulate it to drain funds or bypass fee collection.
findings.suggestion: Derive fee amounts from datum fields or protocol parameters, not from the redeemer. If redeemer-based, validate bounds: `expect redeemer.fee >= min_fee && redeemer.fee <= max_fee`.

findings.title: Incomplete Value check via quantity_of in manage_positions.spend
findings.description: Handler manage_positions.spend uses quantity_of to check specific asset quantities but doesn't validate the full Value. Other native assets may be drained.
findings.suggestion: Complement quantity_of checks with assets.match for full Value validation.

findings.title: Incomplete Value check via quantity_of in position_mint.mint
findings.description: Handler position_mint.mint uses quantity_of to check specific asset quantities but doesn't validate the full Value. Other native assets may be drained.
findings.suggestion: Complement quantity_of checks with assets.match for full Value validation.

findings.title: State machine issue: Close in manage_positions.spend
findings.description: Terminal action 'Close' doesn't access mint field (may need token burn)
findings.suggestion: Access the mint field and verify tokens are burned.

findings.title: Handler manage_positions.spend produces outputs without minimum ADA check
findings.description: Handler creates outputs but doesn't verify the minimum ADA (lovelace) requirement. Cardano requires every UTXO to contain a minimum amount of ADA. Note: The Cardano ledger enforces this as a protocol rule — transactions with outputs below the minimum are rejected at submission time, so this is not exploitable but may cause unexpected transaction failures.
findings.suggestion: Ensure outputs include sufficient ADA using `value.from_lovelace(2_000_000)` or check with `value.lovelace_of(output.value) >= min_required`.

findings.title: Handler orders.withdraw produces outputs without minimum ADA check
findings.description: Handler creates outputs but doesn't verify the minimum ADA (lovelace) requirement. Cardano requires every UTXO to contain a minimum amount of ADA. Note: The Cardano ledger enforces this as a protocol rule — transactions with outputs below the minimum are rejected at submission time, so this is not exploitable but may cause unexpected transaction failures.
findings.suggestion: Ensure outputs include sufficient ADA using `value.from_lovelace(2_000_000)` or check with `value.lovelace_of(output.value) >= min_required`.

findings.title: Handler position_mint.mint produces outputs without minimum ADA check
findings.description: Handler creates outputs but doesn't verify the minimum ADA (lovelace) requirement. Cardano requires every UTXO to contain a minimum amount of ADA. Note: The Cardano ledger enforces this as a protocol rule — transactions with outputs below the minimum are rejected at submission time, so this is not exploitable but may cause unexpected transaction failures.
findings.suggestion: Ensure outputs include sufficient ADA using `value.from_lovelace(2_000_000)` or check with `value.lovelace_of(output.value) >= min_required`.

findings.title: Unreachable function 'policy_in_value'
findings.description: Function 'policy_in_value' in module 'utils' is not reachable from any validator entry point via the call graph.
findings.suggestion: Remove the unused function or verify it is called through a dynamic pattern not captured by static analysis.

findings.title: Unreachable function 'calculate_rebalance_asset_sent_to_pool'
findings.description: Function 'calculate_rebalance_asset_sent_to_pool' in module 'math' is not reachable from any validator entry point via the call graph.
findings.suggestion: Remove the unused function or verify it is called through a dynamic pattern not captured by static analysis.

findings.title: Validator 'position_mint' has 6 parameters (max recommended: 4)
findings.description: Parameters: orders_script_hash, t_manage_positions_script_hash, pool_nft_policy_id, pool_nft_asset_name, settings_nft_policy_id, settings_nft_asset_name. Consider grouping related parameters.
findings.suggestion: Group related parameters into a configuration datum or reference script.

findings.title: No function calls in always_fail.spend — possible unused imports
findings.description: Handler 'always_fail.spend' references variables but calls no functions. Most validators use stdlib functions (list.has, interval checks, etc). This may indicate unused imports or missing validation logic.
findings.suggestion: Review imports and add the expected validation logic.

findings.title: Multiple quantity_of calls in position_mint.mint may allow double-counting
findings.description: Handler position_mint.mint calls `quantity_of` 5 times to check asset quantities. Without verifying mutual exclusivity, an attacker may satisfy multiple checks with overlapping assets.
findings.suggestion: Use `value.tokens` or `value.without_lovelace` for exact value matching instead of multiple independent quantity_of checks.
